title: Zcashd 5.6.0 released, SDKs and lightwalletd next
contentSnippet: Electric Coin Co. released zcashd 5.6.0 today, a key piece to solving wallet performance issues and one of the last remaining hurdles to exiting emergency mode. The SDK and lightwalletd will be released next week.

title: Zcash Foundation releases first stable, audited version of Zebra
contentSnippet: Zebra is an alternative Zcash node implementation that has been written in entirely in RUST. It can be used to join the Zcash peer-to-peer network, validate and broadcast transactions, and maintain the Zcash blockchain state in a distributed manner.

title: Shielded Labs receives funding to bootstrap Zcash work
contentSnippet: An independent, Swiss-based organization called Shielded Labs announced it received a generous private donation to bootstrap operations. Its mission is to increase user adoption, develop new use cases for Zcash, and contribute to protocol development.

title: Zooko featured in ‘A brief history of zero-knowledge proofs’
contentSnippet: The past, present, and future of zero-knowledge proofs. Featuring Electric Coin Co. CEO Zooko Wilcox.

title: Speeding up FROST with multi-scalar multiplication
contentSnippet: Zcash Foundation optimizes implementation of FROST by upwards of 50% over the trivial implementation, without changing the protocol and therefore maintaining its existing security guarantees.

title: ECC restructuring to better serve Zcash
contentSnippet: Electric Coin Co. announced a restructuring of the company to focus its time and expertise on specific parts of the Zcash mission and help transition some activities to other organizations in the Zcash community.

title: Ledger joins PGP for Crypto podcast
contentSnippet: Ledger's Seth Hertlein, Global Head of Policy, and Ian Rogers, Chief Experience Officer, sit down with ECC's Gary Weinstein to discuss blockchain policy, crypto regulation, and global public policy strategy. Special emphasis on key regulatory challenges in the US and EU.

title: Toward a Marshall Plan for Ukraine
ECC’s Brigner joins Federalist Society panel on CBDCs
contentSnippet: This webinar features a discussion with US Rep. Tom Emmer, Prof. J.W. Verret of the Antonin Scalia Law School, and an expert panel discussing a Federal Reserve CBDC the recent sanctioning of Tornado Cash and more.

contentSnippet: Former European integration minister Gordana Djurovic tells BIRN that Montenegro, the frontrunner for EU accession, now has membership within its sights - but cautions that there is plenty of hard work ahead.

title: Belgrade Slams Terrorism Conviction of Serbs; Kosovo MPs Applaud Verdict
contentSnippet: The Serbian government condemned the verdict convicting three Serbs of involvement in a deadly attack in northern Kosovo in 2023, but MPs in Pristina burst into applause when they heard about the judgment.

title: Kosovo MPs Unanimously Ratify Membership of Trump’s Board of Peace
contentSnippet: Kosovo MPs supported a bill to ratify the country’s membership of President Donald Trump’s ‘Board of Peace’, with lawmakers saying it helps the Balkan state get closer to the US.

title: Pots and kettles? PM Siliņa is not only Latvian politician to use airport VIP lounges
contentSnippet: The use of airport VIP lounges and associated facilities –&nbsp;which have been the focus for attacks on the judgment of Prime Minister Evika Siliņa by opposition parties – turns out to be far from&nbsp; &nbsp; the exclusive preserve of Siliņa and her New Unity party, it has emerged.

title: Concerts by Latvian and US military orchestras set for Aizkraukle and Rīga
contentSnippet: From May 2 to 6, the United States Air Forces Europe and Africa Orchestra will visit Latvia to perform joint concerts with the Latvian National Guard Orchestra.

title: Three for the weekend: Vasks, Valka, Talka
contentSnippet: The Spring is officially here – which is something we seem to have been repeating for several months now, perhaps more in hope than true belief. Nevertheless, there can be no clearer indications than some of the things taking place this weekend – despite the possibility of snowfall.&nbsp;

title: Kosovo Finds Three Serbs Guilty of 2023 Armed Attack in Banjska
contentSnippet: Three Serbs were found guilty of terrorism and involvement in a violent attempt to seize northern parts of Kosovo in September 2023, as part of an armed group that was backed by Serbia.

title: Alūksne poultry farm to issue EUR 30 million in bonds
contentSnippet: The owner of "Alūksnes putnu fermas," AS "Agrova Baltics," will issue bonds worth 30 million euros to finance future growth and investment projects, the company announced following a decision by its shareholders.

title: «Le nuage radioactif s’est arrêté à la frontière» : le mythe du mensonge d’État né d’une communication catastrophique après Tchernobyl
contentSnippet: ANALYSE - Contrairement à ce que l’on pense, cette phrase n’a jamais été prononcée. Mais alors, que s’est-il vraiment passé en France dans les jours qui ont suivi la catastrophe pour que l’idée d’avoir été trompés soit encore aujourd’hui si puissamment ancrée dans les esprits ?

title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters
contentSnippet: At a moment of mounting nuclear danger and deepening global mistrust, governments from nearly every country will convene at the United Nations next week to confront a central question: will the world’s main barrier against the spread of nuclear weapons still hold?

title: World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia
contentSnippet: The head of the UN’s civil aviation agency, ICAO, has called for immediate global action to protect commercial flights from escalating military threats, including missiles, drones, and GPS signal jamming.

title: For every generation, vaccines work and they have saved over 150 million lives: WHO
contentSnippet: Over the past 50 years, vaccines have saved more than&nbsp;150 million lives, as ordinary people chose to protect themselves, their&nbsp;children&nbsp;and their communities from diseases like measles, diphtheria, pertussis, polio, and rotavirus.&nbsp;

title: As Gaza misery continues, shortages in Iran begin to bite
contentSnippet: Desperate and dangerous conditions in Gaza continue to hamper recovery efforts for the wartorn enclave's people, the UN health agency said on Friday, while demining experts warned that they’ve “barely scratched the surface” in assessing the level of contamination of unexploded ordnance.

title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended as humanitarian concerns persist
contentSnippet: The United States has announced that the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire will be extended by three weeks following talks in Washington, offering a potential pause in hostilities. Meanwhile, humanitarian concerns are growing over shortages in Iran and Gaza, while another UN peacekeeper has died after coming

title: Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries
contentSnippet: A growing share of global hunger is becoming entrenched in a small group of conflict-hit countries, with two-thirds of people facing acute food insecurity concentrated in just 10 nations, a major international report backed by UN agencies warns.

title: Latvian plywood producer expands Rēzekne factory
contentSnippet: On Friday, April 24, "Verems," a subsidiary of plywood producer "Latvijas finieris," will open a new production facility in the Rēzekne region.

title: ARTE Europe Weekly: Is Bulgaria’s Election A Gamechanger?
contentSnippet: Will the clear victory for Rumen Radev’s Progressive Bulgaria in last weekend's elections herald new efforts to tackle corruption, as he has promised - or cause new problems in the country's relationship with the EU?

title: Studying in Rīga is a major financial challenge for students from the regions
contentSnippet: A ninety-minute lecture in Riga may mean a five-hour bus ride for a student. For someone else, finding affordable rental housing in the capital at current market prices may mean struggling to pay for meals. There are warnings that higher education in the capital is in danger of becoming a privilege

title: Online e-book buying not particularly popular in Latvia

contentSnippet: The paths of a street-vendor from Kandahar, an elderly couple from Bulgaria, and a Greek tech worker and a tattooist converge on an Aegean holiday island with a troubled past.
